game_info.title: Untitled Game
game_info.concept: **Bob Esponja: The Cosmic Shake:** Players control SpongeBob in a 3D platforming adventure, navigating 7 distinct Wish Worlds using a virtual joystick for movement on the left side of the screen and context-sensitive action buttons on the right for jumping, attacking, and interacting. Progression involves unlocking new traversal abilities, such as a "Karate Kick" to break specific barriers or a "Hook-Swing" to cross large gaps, which are granted upon completing story quests or defeating boss enemies. These abilities are crucial for accessing previously unreachable areas and solving environmental puzzles, with difficulty scaling through more intricate platforming challenges and enemies requiring specific ability counters. Players can collect a unique in-game currency to unlock over 30 cosmetic costumes, offering visual customization without affecting gameplay. The core loop involves exploring worlds, overcoming obstacles, defeating enemies, and completing objectives to advance the narrative, with failure occurring upon losing all 3 health points or falling into hazards, respawning at the last checkpoint.
game_info.description: Guide SpongeBob through cosmic platforming challenges across mystical wish worlds! Navigate treacherous platforms, defeat enemies, and collect cosmic coins. Unlock special abilities like Karate Kick and Hook-Swing to access new areas and overcome obstacles. Complete objectives, find checkpoints, and reach the portal to advance. Manage your 3 hearts carefully - fall into hazards or lose all health, and you'll respawn at the last checkpoint!
game_info.controls:
Arrow Keys / WASD: Move SpongeBob left and right
Space: Jump (double jump when unlocked)
Z: Karate Kick attack / Break barriers
Shift: Hook-Swing (when near swing points and unlocked)
Enter: Start game from menu
Escape: Pause/Unpause game
R: Restart from game over screen

TEST_1.test_description: Testing basic movement and jump mechanics. Validates that SpongeBob can move left/right, jump once initially, and properly interact with platforms using Matter.js physics. Tests ground detection and basic collision response.
TEST_1.strategy_description: Automated input will alternate between left/right movement for 3 seconds each, then perform jumps at regular intervals. The test verifies position changes, velocity constraints, and collision with ground platforms.
TEST_1.expected_outcome: SpongeBob should smoothly move horizontally, jump with realistic physics, land on platforms without clipping through, and maintain proper velocity bounds. Position logs should show expected coordinate changes. Test succeeds if no physics errors occur after 300 frames.

TEST_2.test_description: Testing win condition and ability unlocking progression. Simulates optimal gameplay where the player collects coins, unlocks abilities through progression, and reaches the end portal to win the game.
TEST_2.strategy_description: Automated sequence moves SpongeBob right continuously, jumps over gaps, collects coins, automatically unlocks abilities when score thresholds are met, uses Karate Kick on barriers, and reaches the end portal trigger.
TEST_2.expected_outcome: Game should transition through ability unlocks (double jump at 50 coins, hook-swing at 100), break barriers with Karate Kick, and trigger GAME_OVER_WIN when reaching portal with all abilities. Score should reach target threshold and gamePhase should change to GAME_OVER_WIN within 600 frames.

TEST_3.test_description: Testing damage system and health management. Validates that enemy collisions reduce health, player can take 3 hits before game over, and invincibility frames prevent damage stacking.
TEST_3.strategy_description: Move SpongeBob into enemy collision zones intentionally. Test that each collision reduces health by 1 heart, invincibility period activates (visual feedback), and losing all 3 hearts triggers GAME_OVER_LOSE.
TEST_3.expected_outcome: Health should decrease from 3 to 0 over 3 separate enemy collisions with invincibility gaps. Game should transition to GAME_OVER_LOSE when health reaches 0. Invincibility should prevent immediate re-damage.

TEST_4.test_description: Testing hazard death and checkpoint respawn system. Validates that falling below screen or hitting hazards triggers respawn at last activated checkpoint with health penalty.
TEST_4.strategy_description: Guide SpongeBob to fall off platform edge into void area. Test that position resets to checkpoint location, health decreases by 1, and physics state reinitializes properly.
TEST_4.expected_outcome: When y-position exceeds screen bounds, SpongeBob should teleport to last checkpoint coordinates with velocity reset. Health decreases but game continues until all hearts lost. Checkpoint system should maintain progress.

TEST_5.test_description: Testing Karate Kick ability and barrier destruction. Validates that pressing Z with unlocked ability destroys barriers in range, creating new paths forward.
TEST_5.strategy_description: Unlock Karate Kick through coin collection, position SpongeBob near barrier objects, execute Z key input, verify barrier entity removal from world and entities array.
TEST_5.expected_outcome: Barriers marked as breakable should be removed from Matter.js world when SpongeBob uses Karate Kick in proximity. Visual feedback shows attack animation, barrier disappears, and path becomes accessible.
TEST_5.description: Testing Karate Kick ability and barrier destruction. Validates that pressing Z with unlocked ability destroys barriers in range, creating new paths forward.

TEST_5.expected: Barriers marked as breakable should be removed from Matter.js world when SpongeBob uses Karate Kick in proximity. Visual feedback shows attack animation, barrier disappears, and path becomes accessible.

TEST_6.test_description: Testing Hook-Swing mechanic for gap traversal. Validates that holding Shift near swing points propels SpongeBob across large gaps when ability is unlocked.
TEST_6.strategy_description: Position SpongeBob near hook-swing marker, press and hold Shift key, verify velocity boost in horizontal direction carries SpongeBob across gap to far platform.
TEST_6.expected_outcome: When Shift pressed near swing point with ability unlocked, SpongeBob receives momentum boost (Body.setVelocity) in forward direction. Successfully crosses gap that's otherwise unjumpable, landing safely on opposite platform.
TEST_6.description: Testing Hook-Swing mechanic for gap traversal. Validates that holding Shift near swing points propels SpongeBob across large gaps when ability is unlocked.

TEST_7.test_description: Testing pause/resume functionality and game state persistence. Validates that ESC key properly freezes game physics, renders pause overlay, and resumes without state corruption.
TEST_7.strategy_description: During active gameplay, press ESC to pause. Verify Matter.js engine stops updating, game entities maintain positions, pause UI displays. Press ESC again to resume and verify physics continues from exact saved state.
TEST_7.expected_outcome: GamePhase transitions between PLAYING and PAUSED correctly. All entity positions, velocities, and game state variables persist through pause. Physics simulation freezes during pause and resumes seamlessly without glitches or position jumps.
TEST_7.description: Testing pause/resume functionality and game state persistence. Validates that ESC key properly freezes game physics, renders pause overlay, and resumes without state corruption.
TEST_7.strategy: During active gameplay, press ESC to pause. Verify Matter.js engine stops updating, game entities maintain positions, pause UI displays. Press ESC again to resume and verify physics continues from exact saved state.
